title: Bug fixes (3) + Google Trends fear/greed sentiment integration

bugs_found: fundamentals.py: Piotroski F-Score max_score=9 but implementation only covers 7 criteria (missing ROA trend, leverage trend, share dilution) — 'strong' threshold was nearly unreachable
survival_model.py: purge_gap computed from config on line 134 but never used — line 135 hardcodes 252 instead of using the config value

liquidity_risk.py: compute_liquidity_metrics passes volume.iloc[1:] and close.iloc[1:] to compute_amihud_illiquidity, but returns uses .dropna() which may drop additional rows — index misalignment risk
observation.gap_identified: Google Trends fear/greed sentiment service (trends_sentiment.py) exists but has no API endpoint and is not wired into market-status or signal engine

bugs_fixed: Fixed Piotroski max_score from 9 to 7 and adjusted 'strong' threshold from >=7 to >=6 to match actual implementation
Fixed survival_model.py to use config-driven purge_gap instead of hardcoded 252
Fixed liquidity_risk.py to use .loc[returns.index] for guaranteed index alignment
implementation.feature_built: Full Google Trends fear/greed integration: (1) New analytics endpoints GET /api/analytics/trends-sentiment and GET /api/analytics/trends-sentiment/{ticker}, (2) Trends sentiment added to market-status endpoint response, (3) trends_fear_greed parameter added to signal engine get_market_signal() as contrarian sentiment component, (4) Wired into market signal computation in market router, (5) Frontend API types and functions added (TrendsSentiment, TrendsSentimentFull, TickerAttention, getTrendsSentiment, getTickerAttention)

limitations: Google Trends integration depends on pytrends which can be rate-limited by Google
trends_sentiment signal has no config weight in regime_signal_weights — uses additive contribution without weight normalization, so its impact on composite signal is secondary

The signal interpretation in trends_sentiment.py has a semantic inconsistency: extreme fear produces a negative signal value but the docstring describes it as a 'contrarian buy' — the signal engine correctly treats positive values as bullish
assessment.self_critique: Could have also fixed the signal interpretation inconsistency in trends_sentiment.py where the sign convention of the 'signal' field contradicts the contrarian interpretation. The market router integration passes the raw signal value which means high fear (positive fg_ratio) becomes negative signal, then positive in the signal engine via the contrarian clamp — the double negation works but is confusing.

next_steps: Add trends_sentiment weight to signal_weights and regime_signal_weights configs for proper normalization
Fix signal sign convention in trends_sentiment.py to match contrarian intent directly
Add VIX term structure integration into regime detection
Wire liquidity risk into portfolio optimizer for liquidity-adjusted position sizing
Add factor decomposition display to frontend stock pages
